title: Where Will The Next Shortage Be?
text:
Where Will The Next Shortage Be?

Louis-Vincent Gave

Gavekal Research | 16 Jul 2026

The natural state of capitalism is the deflationary boom; if only because every entrepreneur, CEO and business leader wakes up every morning wondering how to produce more with less (whether workers, energy, land, commodity inputs…). But this constant drive does not mean that the system always stays in a deflationary boom. Events that can trigger an inflationary backdrop include:The 2018 onset of the US-China trade war and later semiconductor embargo were the first major signposts that our deflationary world had taken an inflationary turn (see our 2019 book Clash of Empires). Since then, events—from the lockdowns and the fiscal and monetary responses to Covid, the Russia-Ukraine war, China’s weaponization of rare earths, the Trump tariffs and the closure of the Strait of Hormuz—have added inflationary fuel to the underlying fire.
